title: malus bhutanica
intro: Malus bhutanica, also known as the Bhutan Crabapple, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Malus bhutanica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural antiseptic. Malus bhutanica can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
